text:
Middle-distance superstar Jakob Ingebrigtsen was forced to eat his words at theOlympicson Tuesday night after losing out to Josh Kerr by two positions in the 1500m final. The Norwegian previously stated that he could beat the British athlete ‘blindfolded’.

Ingebrigtsen and Kerr’s big showdown was a long time in the making. The pair have been trading blows on and off the track for years, with both parties playing up the rivalry in the build-up to the Paris Olympics and making it clear that there is little love between them.

After Kerr got the better of Ingebrigtsen at the Diamond League meeting in Eugene back in May, the Edinburgh-born runner took a break from competing in the 1500m, instead switching his attention to the 800m.

"It is difficult to refer to him as a rival when he is never there,” Ingebrigtsen prodded. “He is known as the Brit who never competes. I try to enter as many races as I can and I want to be here to entertain."

This wasn’t the end of the fighting talk though. When Kerr set the indoor two-mile world record at the Millrose Games back in February, Ingebrigtsen stated: “I would have beaten him in that race, blindfolded.” Since then, attention has been focused on their showdown in Paris.

With the stage set for an all-time classic at the Stade de France, Ingebrigtsen imposed a blistering early pace. The 23-year-old turned the screw as the likes of Kerr and Timothy Cheruiyot clung on behind him. However, the ferocious start proved costly for the reigning 5000m Olympic champion.



Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

As the field rounded onto the home straight on the final lap, Kerr snatched the lead on the outside. Ingebrigtsen moved across to block his rival’s advance but had nothing left in the tank. That defensive move opened the door for Team USA’s Cole Hocker, who thrust himself into the picture with a perfect kick.

After outsprinting Kerr in the final stretch, Hocker sealed an unlikely gold with Team USA colleague Yared Nuguse snatching the bronze medal with a bold late charge of his own. The Brit was second; Ingebrigtsen finished an unfathomable fourth.

“I opened with a 54-second lap,” Ingebrigtsen confessed. “That wasn’t the plan at all. It was at least two seconds too fast. I was thinking about slowing down, but the next lap was almost the same speed. I ruined it for myself by going way too hard.

“I felt really strong and I have had a good progression with races this year. I didn’t realise what pace it actually was. It was not the plan to open that hard.”

title: Josh Kerr's bitter Olympics rival Jakob Ingebrigtsen left with egg on face in epic final | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Harry Smith
description: Josh Kerr and Jakob Ingebrigtsen went toe-to-toe in a blockbuster 1500m final at the Stade de France on Tuesday.
